title: Add 4th called-shot field: ordering rationale
description:
The current 3-field called shot (Test name / Behavior under test / Expected failure) is necessary but not sufficient. It does not force the agent to reason about test ordering relative to implementation forcing before committing to a test. A 4th field requiring explicit ordering rationale would prevent happy-path-first incidents by surfacing the vacuous-green risk before the test is written.

Proposed 4th field: 'Why this test first: [why this is the most conditionally interesting test / what implementation writing this test would force]'

Files to change:
- CLAUDE.md: 'state all three' becomes 'state all four'
- 2. Do/2. Test Drive the Change.md: called shot definition
- eval/rubrics/rubric_2.py: add 4th field to must_contain and evaluation criteria
- eval/scenarios/2_scenarios.json: update called_shot_required or must_contain signals
- Human Working Agreements.md (if it defines called shot format)

Insight source: separate coding session -- 'if I could change one thing: make the called shot require an explicit statement of why this test is the most conditionally interesting one for the node. That extra question would have prevented both happy-path-first incidents.'

title: Update DO phase prompt text with stub discipline and conditional-first test selection
description: Add explicit documentation to 2. Test Drive the Change.md: (1) Stub Design paragraph -- stubs return hardcoded values, never conditional logic; if making a test GREEN requires a conditional in the stub, delete and re-stub. (2) Test Selection paragraph -- when a feature has conditional branches, start with the test that requires those branches, not the happy path. These rules are already exhibited by Sonnet 4.6 in focused scenarios, so the changes serve as documentation and regression protection. Must not degrade existing DO phase eval scores.
close_reason: All DO phase evals passing after prompt changes

title: Run baseline eval with new conditional scenario before prompt changes
description: After adding the ordering-triggered vacuous green scenario to 2_scenarios.json, run the DO phase evals to confirm it scores LOW (0.4-0.7) against the current prompt -- verifying that the current prompt does not prevent the anti-pattern. This baseline score is the RED we need before changing the prompts.
close_reason: Clean baseline: 0/3 shots issued bd update --append-notes. Agent acknowledges beads in prose but never records TDD ordering decisions. Mechanical check bd update fails consistently. GEval 0.20 across all shots. This is the RED we need before updating do-beads-addon.md.

title: Add eval scenario for ordering-triggered vacuous green
description: The existing DO phase eval scenarios (2-first-step, 2-after-passing-test) don't create conditions for Anti-Pattern #7 ordering-triggered sub-case. We need a scenario where: (A) the feature has a clear conditional branch (e.g., escalate flag based on confidence), (B) the stub returns hardcoded empty content, (C) the test list includes both happy-path and conditional tests. Correct behavior: model starts with the conditional test. Incorrect: model starts with happy path and implements everything at once, making the conditional test vacuously green. Without this scenario, rubric criterion #6 is untestable.
close_reason: Scenario 2-conditional-branch-first added to 2_scenarios.json. Empty-content stub, test list has happy path first then escalation-flag conditional. 121 unit tests still passing.

title: Rebuild and run evals to confirm prompt change improvement
description: After updating Testing Anti-Patterns.md and the DO phase prompt, rebuild the skill package (build-skill.sh) and re-run DO phase evals. The new criterion #6 score should be higher than the baseline. All previously passing scenarios must still pass.
close_reason: All DO phase evals passing after prompt changes

title: Run eval baseline with new rubric criterion before prompt changes
description: After adding criterion #6 to rubric_2.py, run the DO phase evals to establish a baseline score. The baseline should be LOW for the new criterion, confirming it is measuring the gap. Record the scores before making any prompt changes.
close_reason: Baseline established. 2-first-step: 0.70, 2-skip-tests-request: n/a (refusal), 2-after-passing-test: 0.90. Key finding: existing scenarios happen to demonstrate correct stub discipline already. New criterion #6 evaluates correctly, but a new scenario is needed to get signal on ordering-triggered vacuous green specifically.

title: Update do-beads-addon.md with TDD ordering capture guidance for conditional-branch features
description: The current do-beads-addon.md teaches task tracking (Before/After/Done-when) but does not instruct the agent to capture TDD test ordering decisions when a feature has conditional branches. Without this, a long DO phase session can drift: the agent knows the full spec but has no persistent record of 'escalation test before happy path to prevent vacuous greens.' Add guidance: when a feature has conditional branches, append a test ordering note to the beads task at the start of the TDD step. This externalizes the decision so bd-show can recover it even after context compaction.
close_reason: All DO phase evals passing after prompt changes

title: Update Testing Anti-Patterns.md with ordering-triggered vacuous green sub-case
description: Anti-Pattern #7 (Vacuous Greens) does not document the ordering-triggered sub-case: writing the happy-path test first can force the stub into a full implementation, making conditional-branch tests vacuously green. Add the sub-case with prevention rule and attribution to afbreilyn/afb-tdd (Happy-Path Tunnel Vision). The do-beads-addon.md change (pdca-3yd) is the mechanism for preventing this at runtime; the anti-patterns doc is the reference that explains why the mechanism exists.
close_reason: All DO phase evals passing after prompt changes

title: Add rubric criterion for stub discipline and conditional-first test selection
description: The DO phase eval rubric (rubric_2.py) does not check whether an agent keeps stubs hardcoded or whether it selects conditional-branch tests before happy-path tests. This gap allows Anti-Pattern #7 (Ordering-Triggered Vacuous Green) to go undetected in evals. Add criterion #6 to CRITERIA and update the 0.7/0.4 score thresholds to catch stubs that grow into conditional implementations and agents that start with happy-path tests when conditionals could be targeted first.
close_reason: rubric_2.py updated with criterion #6 (stub discipline), Stub Discipline evaluation guidance, and updated 0.7/0.4 score thresholds. 121 unit tests still passing.

title: Improve trigger-eval.json with ambiguous realistic scenarios
description: The current 20 trigger scenarios are too obvious: should-trigger cases all explicitly mention PDCA/TDD/systematic. This caused 0% recall during skill-creator optimization runs. Add >= 10 ambiguous should-trigger cases where PDCA applies but the user does not say so (e.g. 'we keep shipping bugs on this service', 'I need to add a payment flow before Friday'). Data file change only -- no code.

title: Add TestBaselineComparison eval test class
description: No tests validate that the skill prompt causes the behaviors rubrics measure. Add TestBaselineComparison in tests/test_evals.py that runs each of the 15 scenarios with and without skill prompt (via include_skill_prompt=False) and asserts positive delta for >= 10 of 15. Requires API key, marked eval. Depends on the executor baseline mode task.

title: Update reporter to display mean +/- stddev and flag high-variance scenarios
description: EvalReporter._render() shows retried scenarios as N/3 shots but has no variance display. Update summary table to show mean +/- stddev for retried scenarios. Add analyst notes section flagging any scenario with stddev > 0.2. TDD: write unit tests for the table column and analyst notes section using mock data, then update reporter.py.

title: Compute shot_mean and shot_stddev in retry result dict
description: When _assert_and_store retries, it stores 3 shots but discards score distribution. Compute shot_mean and shot_stddev from shots[i]['geval_score'] values and store them in the retry result so the reporter can surface variance. TDD: write unit test with a mock result containing known shot scores, then add computation in test_evals.py _assert_and_store.

title: Add include_skill_prompt param to run_phase executor
description: run_phase in eval/executor.py always injects the phase prompt as system context. We need a baseline mode (include_skill_prompt=False) so TestBaselineComparison can run scenarios without the skill prompt and measure the delta. TDD: write unit test first using the existing _client mock injection pattern, then add the param.

title: Rename src/ to pdca-framework/ and flatten core/ directory
description: The claude-skill/src/ directory has accumulated structural debt: src/core/ holds the active build output (SKILL.md + references/), src/beads-addon/sources/ holds active beads source files, and src/beads-addon/SKILL-beads.md + src/beads-addon/references/build/ + src/references/ are orphaned artifacts from the old separate beads skill package. The proposed structure renames src/ to pdca-framework/ and eliminates the core/ indirection level, so the zip root matches the directory name. Requires updating build-skill.sh (CORE_DIR, BEADS_DIR), tests/test_build.py (SKILL_SRC, BEADS_ADDON_DIR), tests/test_evals.py (PHASE_PROMPTS_DIR), and CLAUDE.md documentation. Delete orphaned files (SKILL-beads.md, references/build/, src/references/) as part of the same change.

title: Document skill-creator as trigger accuracy validation tool in Agent.md
description: Agent.md has no guidance on trigger accuracy validation. Add a section explaining that precision/recall on skill invocation is validated by running skill-creator against this skill using eval/trigger-eval.json as scenario input. Include the command and note that trigger-optimization-report.json captures results. Documentation change only.
